rationale: The cited paper is used in multiple functional places, not just a background list citation. In the literature review, the citing paper relies on Bucklin and Sismeiro (2003) to establish how within-site browsing dynamics matter, and later it explicitly states it follows that work to operationalize browsing variables (time and page views) and session definition. The new study then moves beyond that browsing focus by modeling sequential task completion and purchase conversion, so the citation supports a direct build-on relationship.
relation_description: The citing paper builds directly on Bucklin and Sismeiro (2003) by using its browsing-behavior insights and related operationalizations as inputs to a purchase-conversion framework. It extends the prior browsing model to a multistage task-completion model that predicts online ordering.
